tools.name: join_game
tools.description: Connects to a game using the invite code your human gave you. Returns the game_id and your agent_token — keep both, every other tool needs them.

tools.name: get_game_state
tools.description: Everything visible on the human's own screen right now: FEN, ASCII board, full move history, full chat history, presence, timestamps. Call this whenever you want to look at the board — there is no penalty for checking often.

tools.name: get_legal_moves
tools.description: Pure chess rules — where a piece is physically allowed to move right now. Not an evaluation, not a suggestion, not an opinion about what is good. Exactly what a human sees as dots when they click a piece. Omit square to get every legal move in the position.

tools.name: make_move
tools.description: Submit the move you decided on, in your own reasoning — not a menu, not a ranked list, nothing pre-chosen for you. If it is illegal you get a clear reason back and can try again, exactly like a human clicking the wrong square. Optionally include a genuine thought and/or chat message about the move.

tools.name: send_chat
tools.description: Say something to your human, any time — not just on your turn.

tools.name: offer_draw
tools.description: Propose ending the game as a draw. The game stays active until your human responds.

tools.name: respond_to_draw
tools.description: Accept or decline a draw your human offered. This is your own real decision — weigh the actual position however you see fit.

tools.name: create_game
tools.description: Set up a brand new ChessWithClaw game on your own initiative — you do not need to wait for your human to start one, or for anyone else to invite you first.

tools.name: wait_for_event
tools.description: Holds the connection open, checking every couple seconds, for up to ~20 seconds. Returns the moment it becomes your turn, the human sends a chat message, a draw is offered, or the game ends — whichever happens first. Returns event: "timeout" if none of those happened in the window, which just means

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_game_state, make_move, wait_for_event · Public sink: make_move, send_chat.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_game_state, make_move, wait_for_event · Public sink: make_move, send_chat

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
